$description.$value: Core primitive tokens for Nango. Neutral ramp aligned to the actual Nango app charcoal grays (warm-neutral, not zinc). Brand and info both use the Nango cyan-blue ramp (#00B2E3 family — from webapp index.css). Success uses Nango green (#4CD964 family), warning uses Nango yellow (#F7C752 family), danger uses Nango red (#EE4242 family). Geist for UI text, Geist Mono for code/data. Primitives are shared across themes — semantic files resolve them per mode.
Shared primitive color palette sourced from the Nango webapp CSS (packages/webapp/src/index.css).
Absolute anchors.
Transparent for ghost/overlay states.

Warm-neutral charcoal ramp. Light end fixed for clear visual steps: 0=#FFF, 25=#FAFAFA, 50=#F5F5F5, 100=#EBEBEB, 200=#E0E0E0. Dark stops: 975=#000 (canvas), 950=#0B0B0C (page), 925=#121212 (panel), 800=#2A2B2F (overlay). Sourced from Nango webapp charcoal palette.

925-50.$description: neutral.925 at 50% opacity — for alert.bg in dark mode.

975.$description: Extra-dark stop — used for alert/banner bg in dark mode where .950 is too saturated.

$description.$value: Nango brand cyan — the single source of truth for blue in the system. Used for BOTH brand identity (logo, marketing surfaces) AND interactive UI (buttons, links, focus rings, selected states). color.info aliases to color.brand: one blue, two semantic names. Brand 500 (#00B2E3) is the canonical Nango cyan. Update here to shift the entire system's blue.

950-50.$description: brand.950 at 50% opacity — for status.info.bg in dark mode.

975.$description: Extra-dark stop — used for alert/banner bg in dark mode where .950 is too saturated.

$description.$value: Aliased to color.brand. There is one blue in the Nango system — the brand cyan — and color.info exists only as a semantic name for interactive UI (buttons, focus rings, links, selected states, info alerts). Update color.brand to change the blue everywhere. Focus shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0,178,227,0.2).

950-50.$description: brand.950 at 50% opacity — for status.info.bg in dark mode.

975.$description: Extra-dark stop — used for alert/banner bg in dark mode where .950 is too saturated.

$description.$value: Success ramp — connected, healthy, complete states. Sourced from --color-green-* in webapp index.css. 500=#4CD964, 700=#1E9E3E, 900=#0A3D16.

950-50.$description: success.950 at 50% opacity — for status.success.bg in dark mode.

975.$description: Extra-dark stop — used for alert/banner bg in dark mode where .950 is too saturated.

$description.$value: Warning ramp — caution, degraded, retry, non-blocking alerts. Sourced from --color-yellow-* in webapp index.css. 500=#F7C752, 700=#D6A81E, 900=#5C4A0B.

950-50.$description: warning.950 at 50% opacity — for status.warning.bg in dark mode.

975.$description: Extra-dark stop — used for alert/banner bg in dark mode where .950 is too saturated.

$description.$value: Danger ramp — errors, failures, destructive actions. Sourced from --color-red-* in webapp index.css. 500=#EE4242, 700=#E81818, 900=#5A0F0F. Focus shadow for error states: 0 0 0 3px rgba(238,66,66,0.2).

950-50.$description: danger.950 at 50% opacity — for status.danger.bg in dark mode.

Decorative-only accents for charts, avatars, tag differentiation. Sourced from --color-blue/violet/mint/pink/yellow-500 in webapp index.css. Never use for interactive states or status semantics — use info/success/warning/danger for those.

500.$description: Decorative yellow for chart series and categorical data. Same hex as warning.500 by design (Nango brand yellow), but lives in the accent namespace so charts don't reference the semantic warning ramp. Use warning.* only for actual caution/degraded states.

Icon size and stroke primitives.

$description.$value: Focus indicators. Apply outline-default on every focusable component via :focus-visible; swap to outline-danger only for destructive contexts. Same recipe as Light — only the resolved ring colors differ.

primary.$description: Primary button fill — the bright Nango cyan. Pairs with text.onBrand (neutral.900). neutral.900 on cyan.500 = 7.10:1 (AAA ✓). Button shape on neutral.925 = 7.56:1 (UI ✓). Light mode uses cyan.800 + white text; dark mode goes the opposite direction (bright cyan + dark text) because cyan dark enough for white text would be invisible on the dark panel.

primaryHover.$description: Primary hover — one step BRIGHTER (opposite direction from light mode) so the button visibly lifts on hover. neutral.900 on cyan.400 = 8.56:1 (AAA ✓).

primaryActive.$description: Primary pressed — one step darker than default for tactile feedback. neutral.900 on cyan.600 = 5.52:1 (AA ✓).

dangerHover.$description: Destructive hover — one step lighter than danger.700.

dangerActive.$description: Destructive button pressed — danger.800 was 2.62:1 ❌, danger.700 = 4.6:1 ✓.

border.$description: Row separator — alpha.white.6 in dark. Applied as a bottom border on each row.

$description.$value: Tab navigation component tokens. Supports Default (pill), Line (underline), and Vertical orientations. Dark mode is primary — all contrast ratios verified here first.

background.$description: Container trough behind pill tabs. neutral.900 in dark — active pills sit above this.

backgroundLine.$description: Line variant has no container fill — indicator does the work.

border.$description: Optional outer border on the tab list container. alpha.white.10 in dark.

default.$description: Inactive tab — no fill, sits inside the container trough.

hover.$description: Hovered inactive tab. alpha.white.8 in dark.

active.$description: Selected tab pill fill. neutral.850 in dark — lifted above panelMuted (900) container. Contrast delta is intentional and subtle.

disabled.$description: Disabled tab has no fill — communicated via text.disabled only.

default.$description: Inactive tab label. neutral.400 — 4.73:1 on panelMuted (900). ✓ AA.

hover.$description: Hovered tab label. neutral.200 — steps up from secondary on interaction.

active.$description: Selected tab label. neutral.200 — 10.4:1 on overlay (850). ✓ AAA.

disabled.$description: Disabled tab label. neutral.600 — 3.4:1 on canvas. WCAG exempts disabled; acceptable.

active.$description: Subtle border on active pill tab to separate it from the container. alpha.white.16 in dark.

color.$description: Line variant active underline. neutral.200 in dark — matches active tab text, keeps it neutral rather than brand-blue.

colorActive.$description: Same as indicator.color — aliased for Token Studio component binding clarity.

$description.$value: Pagination component tokens — dark mode (Nango primary). Four item types (Previous, Next, Link, Ellipsis) share one set of state tokens. Active state = currently selected page (Link items only — Previous/Next never enter active). Mirrors the tabs pattern: surface.overlay + border.strong on active in dark to lift it visibly above surface.page. All contrast ratios verified here first.

default.$description: Inactive item — transparent. Sits directly on surface.page (neutral.950).

hover.$description: Hovered item pill. alpha.white.8 in dark.

active.$description: Selected page — neutral.850 in dark. Lifted above surface.page (950) by both fill and border. Same pattern as tabs.item.background.active.

activeHover.$description: Hover on the selected page — alpha.white.8 layered over the overlay fill.

disabled.$description: Disabled Previous/Next — no fill. Communicated via text.disabled + icon.disabled.

default.$description: Default item label (Previous, Next, Link). neutral.400 in dark — 7.0:1 on surface.page (950). ✓ AAA.

hover.$description: Hovered/focused label — steps up from secondary. neutral.200 in dark.

active.$description: Selected page label. neutral.200 in dark — 10.4:1 on surface.overlay (850). ✓ AAA.

disabled.$description: Disabled label (e.g. Previous on first page). neutral.600 — WCAG-exempt for disabled.

ellipsis.$description: Non-interactive ellipsis (…). Muted — signals it's not a button.

default.$description: Chevron in Previous/Next at rest. icon/secondary — neutral.450 in dark.

hover.$description: Chevron on hover/focus — steps up to icon/default. neutral.300 in dark.

active.$description: Active chevron. Previous/Next never enter the active page state, but defined for consistency.

disabled.$description: Disabled chevron — neutral.600 in dark.

default.$description: No border on inactive items.

active.$description: Border on the selected page in dark — alpha.white.16. Stronger than light's border.default to disambiguate the lifted overlay from surface.page (which is a closer step in dark). Same role as tabs.item.border.active.

$description.$value: Standalone Toggle component tokens — a single two-state button that can be on or off. Two variants: Default (ghost, borderless) and Outline (always-visible border). Three sizes: sm, md, lg. Dark mode is primary — all contrast ratios verified here first.
Background fills. Shared across Default and Outline variants — the variants only differ in border treatment.

offHover.$description: Hover on off state. alpha.white.8 in dark — composes onto any surface.

on.$description: On/active. neutral.850 in dark — clearly lifted above page (950) and panel (925). Matches shadcn neutral-selected pattern.

onHover.$description: On + hover. Stable — depth already established, further change creates flicker.

disabled.$description: Disabled — communicated via dimmed text/icon, not fill.

off.$description: Off state label. neutral.400 — 7.34:1 on panel (925) ✓ AAA.

offHover.$description: Hover on off state — steps up from secondary.

on.$description: On state label. neutral.200 — 12.20:1 on overlay (850) ✓ AAA.

disabled.$description: Disabled label. neutral.600 — WCAG-exempt.

on.$description: On state icon.

$description.$value: Border tokens separated by variant. Default variant is borderless (ghost), Outline variant always has a visible border. Focus ring is shared.

off.$description: Default variant — ghost button, no border.

on.$description: Default variant stays borderless when on — fill carries the state.

off.$description: Outline variant — always visible. alpha.white.10 in dark.

on.$description: On state — stronger border reads cleanly against the lifted overlay fill in dark.

disabled.$description: Disabled outline border.

$description.$value: Toggle Group component tokens. A set of two-state buttons grouped horizontally or vertically. Supports Default (connected, no trough), Fill (container with trough, equal-width items), and With Spacing (detached items) variants. Dark mode is primary — all contrast ratios verified here first.
Container wrapper around toggle items. Background only rendered for the Fill variant.

default.$description: Default + With Spacing variants have no container fill — items carry the visual structure.

fill.$description: Fill variant trough. neutral.900 in dark — active item (overlay 850) sits above it. Same relationship as tabs.list.background.

disabled.$description: Whole group disabled fill. neutral.900 in dark.

default.$description: Outer border on Default and Fill containers. alpha.white.10 in dark — hairline separator.

spacing.$description: With Spacing variant has no outer container border — each item carries its own.

$description.$value: Individual toggle button within the group. Off = inactive, On = selected/pressed.

off.$description: Inactive item — no fill. Items in the Fill variant rest on panelMuted trough; Default/Spacing items rest on page bg.

offHover.$description: Hover on inactive item. alpha.white.8 in dark — composes onto any surface.

on.$description: Selected (on) item. neutral.850 in dark — lifted above panelMuted (900) trough. Matches tabs.item.background.active and the shadcn neutral-selected pattern.

onHover.$description: Selected + hover — stable. Depth is already established; further fill change creates flicker.

disabled.$description: Disabled item — communicated via dimmed text/icon, not fill.

off.$description: Inactive item label. neutral.400 — 4.73:1 on panelMuted (900) ✓ AA.

offHover.$description: Hovered inactive item label — steps up from secondary.

on.$description: Selected item label. neutral.200 — 10.4:1 on overlay (850) ✓ AAA.

disabled.$description: Disabled item label. neutral.600 — 3.4:1 on canvas. WCAG exempts disabled; acceptable.

off.$description: Inactive item icon. Matches text.secondary tone.

offHover.$description: Hovered inactive item icon — steps up.

on.$description: Selected item icon — primary icon tone.

disabled.$description: Disabled item icon.

default.$description: Item border for Default (connected) and With Spacing (detached) variants. alpha.white.10 in dark.

on.$description: Selected item border — slightly stronger to define the lifted surface edge in dark mode. alpha.white.16.

fill.$description: Items in Fill variant have no border — container trough + fill separation do the work.

$description.$value: Component tokens for selection controls such as checkbox, radio, and switch, built on top of existing semantic dark tokens.

invalid.$description: Unselected+invalid: bg stays transparent; the border carries the invalid signal.

dangerFill.$description: Danger-filled bg for a selected+invalid control (e.g. checked checkbox in error state).

invalid.$description: Invalid state border — danger.500 in light, danger.500 in dark.

dangerHover.$description: Hover border on invalid control — darker danger stop.

invalid.$description: Icon/checkmark color in invalid unselected state.

$description.$value: Checkbox-specific aliases.

dangerFill.$description: Danger fill for checked+invalid checkbox. References control.bg.dangerFill.

invalidSelected.$description: Checkmark on danger fill — stays white (onBrand) for legibility.

$description.$value: Box variant — wraps control + label + description in a bordered card.

invalid.$description: Decorative tint only — not a text contrast pair.

$description.$value: Radio-specific aliases.
Box variant — wraps radio + label + description in a bordered card.
Switch-specific aliases for track and thumb.

dangerOff.$description: Invalid+Off track. Same as off state — border carries the danger signal.

dangerOn.$description: Invalid+On track turns danger instead of info.

invalid.$description: Outline ring applied to entire switch track when invalid.

$description.$value: Box variant — wraps switch + label + description in a bordered card.

on.$description: Box bg when switch is On — subtle info tint matches selected state.

$description.$value: Alert and toast banner component tokens. Aliases into status.* — mode-specific colors resolved via Primitives.

Chart component tokens — used by usage charts, dashboards, and metric panels (e.g. Billing & usage page). Categorical stacked bars use chart.series.1–6 (decorative-only colours from accent.* + info). Status-based charts should reference status.* colours directly. Both modes share the same token names — series colours stay perceptually similar across themes since they're saturated mid-tones that read on both light and dark surfaces.
Chart container — the bordered card that wraps the title, value, and plot area.

background.$description: Chart panel background — neutral.925 in dark.

border.$description: Hairline border around the chart panel — alpha.white.10 in dark.

title.$description: Chart title (e.g. ‘Connections’, ‘Proxy requests’). neutral.200 in dark. Pair with fontSize.md + fontWeight.medium at the Figma text-style level.

value.$description: Large numeric total under the title (e.g. ‘453’, ‘5,453,937’). neutral.50 in dark. Pair with fontSize.xl + fontWeight.semibold.

$description.$value: Axis labels and lines along the X (categories/days) and Y (values) edges of the plot area.

label.$description: Axis tick labels — small, low-emphasis. neutral.400 in dark. Pair with fontSize.2xs.

line.$description: Axis baseline. Transparent by default — gridlines do the work. Switch to {border.muted} if a visible axis line is required.

gridline.$description: Horizontal gridlines behind the bars. alpha.white.8 in dark. Render as a 1px hairline (or dashed if preferred).

1.$description: Primary series — Nango cyan (#00B2E3). First/dominant category in any breakdown.

2.$description: Mint (#6ED6AC). Pairs visually with cyan without competing.

3.$description: Yellow (#F7C752). Decorative — not a warning. Use for the third stacked category, never to communicate caution.

6.$description: Soft blue (#90A1F0). Reserve slot — sixth category. Distinct from the cyan in series.1.

$description.$value: Categorical data series — assigned in order: 1 first, 2 second, etc. Six slots cover the breakdown views in Billing & usage. If a chart needs more than six categories, repeat from 1 with a tonal shift or switch to a sequential ramp. Keep the assignment stable per chart so colours don't shift between renders.
Per-bar styling — applied to each segment in a stacked bar.

separator.$description: 1px line between stacked segments. Same colour as panel background (neutral.925 in dark) — creates a clean visual gap between segments without a hard border colour.

$description.$value: Tooltip shown on bar hover — surfaces exact values and series labels.

background.$description: Tooltip card background — neutral.850 in dark.

title.$description: Tooltip header — the date or category label of the hovered bar.

label.$description: Series name in a tooltip row.

value.$description: Numeric value in a tooltip row.

$description.$value: Legend — colour swatch + label per series. Optional in the current usage page (the breakdown dropdown handles series assignment), but defined here so it's available when a multi-chart view needs one.
Empty state — shown inside the chart panel when there's no data for the selected period.

text.$description: Empty-state message colour (e.g. ‘No data for this period’).

background.$description: No background — the empty state inherits the chart panel surface.

$description.$value: Semantic light mode tokens. All components reference these — never reference Primitives directly. Simplified for scale: no component tokens except button and input. State tokens use alpha overlays so they work on any background.
Background fills for layout surfaces. Use these on frames, panels, and containers.

panelMuted.$description: Secondary panels, table headers, muted sections.

panelInset.$description: Inset wells, code blocks, inline backgrounds.

overlay.$description: Modals, popovers, dropdowns — floats above page.

inputMuted.$description: Muted/readonly input background. Also used as the off-state track fill for Switch.

$description.$value: Interaction state overlays. Alpha-based so they compose correctly on any surface.

hover.$description: Hover state — apply as fill over any surface.

selected.$description: Selected item background — nav, list, table row.

disabled.$description: Disabled element background.

$description.$value: Text colours. Use the most appropriate semantic level — never use primitives directly on text.

default.$description: Standard body text.

disabled.$description: Disabled text.

brand.$description: Brand-coloured text (Nango cyan) — use sparingly, for brand identity moments only. Uses brand.800 in light to pass WCAG AA on white (6.31:1); brand.700 (#0089B0) fails at 4.03:1. NOT for links or interactive text — use text.link / text.linkHover instead.

$description.$value: Border and divider colours.

default.$description: Standard border — cards, inputs, dividers.

selected.$description: Selected item border — active nav indicator.

disabled.$description: Disabled element border.

$description.$value: Icon fill colours. Match to the surrounding text hierarchy.

default.$description: Primary icon.

disabled.$description: Disabled icon.

brand.$description: Brand-coloured icon (Nango cyan) — for brand identity moments. NOT for interactive icons — use icon.default or icon.info instead.

$description.$value: Focus indicators. Apply outline-default on every focusable component via :focus-visible; swap to outline-danger only for destructive contexts (Delete, Disconnect, Revoke, error inputs). Stacked drop shadow: 2px gap matching surface.canvas + 2px ring (focus.ring-default). The two outline tokens are the only focus tokens consumed by components; ring-default and ring-danger exist as theme-aware color aliases so the outline recipe can stay theme-agnostic.

ring-default.$description: Canonical focus ring color (light). Uses info.700, not info.600 — info.600 against surface.canvas measures 2.92:1 and fails WCAG 2.4.11 (3:1).

ring-danger.$description: Focus ring color for destructive contexts (light). 3.89:1 vs surface.canvas.

outline-default.$description: Universal focus ring. Apply to every focusable component (buttons, inputs, cards, table rows, tabs, links). Two-layer drop shadow: 2px gap of surface.canvas + 2px ring of focus.ring-default.

outline-danger.$description: Focus ring for destructive contexts. Apply to destructive buttons and inputs in an error state.

$description.$value: Button and interactive control fills. Components use these directly.

primary.$description: Primary button fill — deep cyan with white text. White text on info.800 (#016886) = 6.31:1 (AA ✓). Cyan ramp at mid stops is too luminous for white text, so light mode uses .800 as the default.

primaryHover.$description: Primary hover — one step darker. White text on info.900 (#02485D) = 10.06:1 (AAA ✓).

primaryActive.$description: Primary pressed — two steps darker for tactile press feedback. White text on info.950 (#013747) = 12.81:1 (AAA ✓).

outline.$description: Outline button — transparent fill, visible border.

ghost.$description: Ghost button — transparent fill, no border.

danger.$description: Destructive button fill. danger.700 ensures 4.6:1 with white text (AA ✓).

disabled.$description: Disabled button fill.

selectedFill.$description: Active indicator fill — left border of selected nav item.

$description.$value: Semantic status tokens for badges, alerts, and indicators.

text.$description: Bumped from .700 to .800 — cyan.700 on info.50 = 3.84:1 fails AA. cyan.800 = 6.01:1 ✓.

icon.$description: Matches status.info.text for visual unity inside alerts.

text.$description: success.800 on success.50 = 6.25:1 (AA ✓). success.700 was 3.31:1 ❌.

icon.$description: Matches status.success.text for visual unity inside alerts.
Matches status.warning.text for visual unity inside alerts.

text.$description: danger.800 on danger.50 = 7.5:1 (AA ✓). danger.700 was 4.28:1 ❌.

icon.$description: Matches status.danger.text for visual unity inside alerts.

$description.$value: Button component tokens. Reference interactive.* and text.* for fills and labels.

default.$description: White text on solid-fill buttons.

$description.$value: Outline button — transparent fill, visible border.

disabled.$description: Keep transparent — disabled state shown via dimmed text+border only.

$description.$value: Ghost button — transparent fill, no border.

disabled.$description: Keep transparent — disabled state shown via dimmed text only.

default.$description: White text on solid-fill buttons.

$description.$value: Link-danger button — no fill, no border. Danger-coloured text signals destructive intent. Use for inline destructive actions (delete, revoke, disconnect) where a solid danger button would be too heavy.

default.$description: No fill at rest — reads as text, not a button.

hover.$description: Neutral alpha hover (alpha.black.6) — keeps danger.800 text at 7.0:1+ on all light surfaces.

active.$description: Neutral alpha press (alpha.black.10).

disabled.$description: Disabled state is communicated by dimmed text only.

default.$description: danger.800 (#A11313) — steps up from text.danger (700) which fails on interactive hover surfaces. 8.02:1 on white, 7.0:1 on state.pressed. ✓ AAA across all surfaces.

disabled.$description: WCAG exempts disabled elements — matches all other disabled text.

default.$description: No border — link-style button.
Matches text color. danger.800 in light for contrast parity.

$description.$value: Secondary button — pure mono inverse. Black fill with white label in light mode (white fill with black label in dark). Reach for it when the page already has a primary blue CTA and you need a second high-emphasis action that feels visually distinct (e.g. 'Connect integration' alongside 'Save changes'). Maximum contrast against any surface in the system — 21:1 in both modes.

default.$description: Pure #000000. Anchored to color.mono.* — never references neutral so it stays absolute across theme tweaks.

hover.$description: neutral.900 (#18191B) — reads as black to the eye but provides tactile lift on hover. White text remains 18.9:1. ✓ AAA.

active.$description: neutral.800 (#2A2B2F) — slightly deeper press feedback. White text 14.1:1. ✓ AAA.

disabled.$description: Shared disabled fill — keeps disabled-state parity with primary and danger buttons.

default.$description: Pure #FFFFFF on pure black — 21:1. ✓ AAA.
No border — fill carries all the visual weight.
Matches label colour.

$description.$value: Badge component tokens. Maps Figma variant names to status.* and surface.* semantics.
Neutral badge — no semantic meaning.
Muted neutral badge — subtle background with a hairline border for definition. Use for environment labels, version numbers, and low-emphasis metadata.

bg.$description: surface.panelInset — neutral.100 in light.

text.$description: neutral.600 in light — 5.04:1 on neutral.100 bg. ✓ AA.

border.$description: neutral.200 in light — subtle edge against the neutral.100 bg.

$description.$value: Outline badge — transparent fill, default border.
Destructive / error badge.
Verified / success badge — connected, healthy, complete.
Ghost badge — no fill, no border, muted text.

Filter badge — selectable chip used to filter lists, catalogs and tables. Two visual variants: 'unselected' (outlined, no fill) and 'selected' (info-tinted fill). Each variant has bg, text, and border slots with default/hover/disabled states.
Default outlined state — no fill, neutral border. Used when the filter is not currently applied.

default.$description: Transparent — respects whatever surface the badge sits on (panel, popover, dropdown).

hover.$description: Subtle alpha overlay on hover — signals interactivity without committing to a color.

$description.$value: Active selected state — info-tinted fill, darker info text. Visually communicates that the filter is currently applied.

default.$description: info.50 — lightest blue tint.

hover.$description: Slightly more saturated than default — indicates 'click to deselect'.

default.$description: info.800 on info.50 — 6.01:1, ✓ AA. (info.700 would be 3.84:1 — fails.)

hover.$description: info.900 on info.100 — 9.58:1, ✓ AAA.

default.$description: Decorative — fill + text carry the selected state; border doesn't need to reach 3:1 on its own.

$description.$value: Input field component tokens.
Shadow tokens for elevated containers.
Spinner loading indicator — circular progress indicator for indeterminate loading states.

track.$description: Background ring — very faint, defines the full arc path. neutral.200 on neutral.0 = 1.3:1 (intentionally subtle — it is a structural cue, not content).

indicator.$description: Animated foreground arc — neutral.700 on neutral.0 = 10.4:1 ✅. Neutral intentionally — spinners appear on varied surfaces.

$description.$value: Dialog component tokens — a window overlaid on the primary window for non-destructive confirmations, forms, and scrollable content. Includes a close button; user may dismiss without acting.

bg.$description: Dialog container background. surface.overlay = neutral.0 in light.

overlay.$description: Background scrim that dims content behind the dialog.

border.$description: Dialog container border — subtle 1px ring.

Dialog subtitle / description below the title.

divider.$description: Divider between accordion items — neutral.100 in light.

border.$description: Panel border — neutral.200 in light.

default.$description: List item background at rest — transparent.

hover.$description: List item hover fill — alpha.black.6 in light.

selected.$description: Selected list item — neutral.100 in light.

disabled.$description: Disabled list item — transparent.

default.$description: Item label — neutral.800 in light.

secondary.$description: Item description (Custom variant) — neutral.600 in light.

disabled.$description: Disabled item label — neutral.400 in light.

$description.$value: Field — a form-field wrapper that composes a label, an input control, and helper/description text. Layout (vertical / horizontal / responsive) is a CSS concern. These tokens cover only the color roles. The control inside a Field always uses input.* tokens directly.
Field label — the text element that names the control.

default.$description: Label at rest — neutral.800 in light.

error.$description: Label when the field is invalid — danger.700 in light.

disabled.$description: Label when the field is disabled — neutral.400 in light.

$description.$value: Helper / description text — appears below the input (Under Input layout) or below the label (Under Label layout). Switches to error colour when Data Invalid is true.

default.$description: Description at rest — neutral.600 in light.

error.$description: Description when field is invalid — danger.700 in light.

disabled.$description: Description when field is disabled — neutral.400 in light.

$description.$value: Field legend — used when a group of controls shares a title and description (fieldset pattern). Two sub-variants: Legend (title-only label) and Label (title + description).

The optional footer row that holds form actions (Submit, Save draft) and auxiliary links (Already have an account? Sign in). Buttons use existing button.* tokens. Only the auxiliary link text is new here.

text.$description: Auxiliary footer copy — neutral.600 in light.

link.$description: Inline link in footer (e.g. 'Sign in') — info.700 in light.

linkHover.$description: Inline link hover — info.800 in light.

$description.$value: Item — a versatile row component for lists, command results, and data displays. Three surface variants (Default / Outline / Muted) × three sizes (default / sm / xs). Leading slot accepts icon, image, avatar, or avatar group. Trailing slot accepts action button, text+button, or icon button.

default.$description: Default variant background — neutral.0 in light. Items in a list sit on surface.panel with muted dividers between them.

outline.$description: Outline variant background — transparent. Border (item.border.outline) provides the container definition.

muted.$description: Muted variant background — neutral.25 in light.

hover.$description: Hover state fill — alpha.black.6 in light. Applies to all variants.

pressed.$description: Pressed / active fill — alpha.black.10 in light.

selected.$description: Selected item fill — neutral.100 in light.

disabled.$description: Disabled item — transparent.

divider.$description: Separator between items in a list — neutral.100 in light. Applied as a bottom border on each row.

outline.$description: Full border on the Outline variant — neutral.200 in light.

muted.$description: Muted variant border — neutral.100 in light.

default.$description: Item title — neutral.800 in light.

disabled.$description: Disabled item title — neutral.400 in light.

default.$description: Item description / sub-label — neutral.600 in light.

disabled.$description: Disabled item description — neutral.400 in light.

$description.$value: Leading slot — the leftmost visual element of the item row. Four types: simple icon (no container), icon with container, image thumbnail, avatar/avatarGroup.

default.$description: Simple icon (no container) — neutral.500 in light.

disabled.$description: Disabled simple icon — neutral.300 in light.

bg.$description: Container background behind a leading icon — neutral.100 in light. Used when the icon needs visual separation from the item bg.

border.$description: Container border — neutral.100 in light.

icon.$description: Icon inside the container — neutral.700 in light.

$description.$value: Trailing slot — the rightmost area of the item row. Types: action button (uses button.outline.*), text + icon button, or standalone icon button.

default.$description: Metadata text in the trailing area (e.g. '3:45') — neutral.600 in light.

disabled.$description: Disabled trailing text — neutral.400 in light.

default.$description: Trailing icon button at rest — neutral.500 in light.

hover.$description: Trailing icon button hover — neutral.700 in light.

disabled.$description: Disabled trailing icon — neutral.300 in light.

activeIndicator.$description: Left-border accent stripe on the active/selected navigation item. info.700 in light (interactive.selectedFill). Apply as a 2px left border on the item row when item.bg.selected is active.

$description.$value: Empty state — a centred content block shown when a view has no data. Composes an illustration (icon, avatar, or avatarGroup), a title, a description, optional CTA buttons, and an optional 'Learn more' link. CTA buttons use existing button.* tokens. This namespace covers only the illustration container and text roles.

bg.$description: Empty state container background — transparent. The empty state inherits the surface it sits on.

$description.$value: The centred graphic element above the title. Can be a plain icon (with optional container), an avatar, or a stacked avatarGroup.

bg.$description: Container background for a plain icon illustration — neutral.100 in light. Gives the icon a visible bounding box without decorative flair. Do not apply to Avatar or AvatarGroup — they supply their own bg.

border.$description: Container border — neutral.100 in light.

icon.$description: Plain icon fill — neutral.500 in light. Intentionally muted — empty states are informative, not alarming.

text.$description: Empty state heading — neutral.900 in light.
Empty state body copy — neutral.600 in light.

default.$description: 'Learn more →' link at rest — info.700 in light.

hover.$description: 'Learn more' link hover — info.800 in light.

active.$description: 'Learn more' link pressed — info.900 in light.

icon.$description: Arrow icon beside the 'Learn more' link — info.600 in light.

$description.$value: Breadcrumb navigation — a horizontal trail of links showing the current page location. The last item is the current page (non-interactive, bold). Supports a collapsed ellipsis ('…') segment and dropdown items (∨ chevron). Tokens confirmed from Figma selection colors: icon/secondary, text/secondary, text/default, icon/default, surface/canvas.

bg.$description: Breadcrumb bar background — transparent. The bar inherits the surface of the page header (surface.canvas or surface.page).

$description.$value: Individual breadcrumb segment — either a clickable ancestor or the current (non-interactive) page.

default.$description: Ancestor breadcrumb at rest — text/secondary confirmed in Figma. neutral.600 in light.

hover.$description: Ancestor breadcrumb on hover — steps up to text/default. neutral.800 in light. Confirmed from Figma selection colors.

pressed.$description: Ancestor breadcrumb pressed — neutral.900 in light.

disabled.$description: Disabled ancestor — neutral.400 in light.

current.$description: Current page label — text/default confirmed in Figma. Rendered bold (fontWeight.semibold) in typography — same token, heavier weight signals non-interactivity. neutral.800 in light.

default.$description: Ancestor item background at rest — transparent.

hover.$description: Ancestor item hover pill — alpha.black.6 in light.

pressed.$description: Ancestor item pressed — alpha.black.10 in light.

icon.$description: ∨ chevron on items that open an environment/page picker. icon/secondary confirmed in Figma. neutral.500 in light.

icon_hover.$description: Dropdown chevron on hover — steps up to icon/default. neutral.700 in light.

color.$description: The › separator icon between breadcrumb items. icon/secondary confirmed in Figma selection colors. neutral.500 in light.

$description.$value: The collapsed '…' segment shown when the trail is too long to display in full.

text.$description: Ellipsis text/icon colour — text/secondary. neutral.600 in light.

icon.$description: Three-dot icon colour — icon/secondary. neutral.500 in light.

default.$description: Ellipsis pill at rest — transparent.

hover.$description: Ellipsis pill hover — alpha.black.6 in light.

pressed.$description: Ellipsis pill pressed — alpha.black.10 in light.

$description.$value: Secondary settings navigation — the vertical list of sections (General, API Keys, Backend…). Left-border active indicator pattern. Active bg is state.selected (neutral.100), not surface.panel — avoids the floating-card look of tabs on a white sidebar.

default.$description: Inactive item — no fill.

hover.$description: Hover fill — alpha.black.6. Subtle on white sidebar.

active.$description: Active/selected section. neutral.100 (#EBEBEB) in light — a light gray tint, not white-on-white. Contrast: text.default = 12.49:1 ✓ AAA.

default.$description: Inactive label. neutral.600 — 7.41:1 on sidebar bg ✓ AAA.

hover.$description: Hovered label steps up to text.default. neutral.800 in light.

active.$description: Active section label. neutral.800 — 12.49:1 on active bg ✓ AAA.
Left-edge accent bar on the active item. info.500 in light. 2px wide.

default.$description: No indicator on inactive items.

$description.$value: App shell navigation tokens — Light mode. Covers the sidebar shell, environment switcher, quota/usage section, and page header bar.
Sidebar panel shell. Fixed left, 220px wide.

bg.$description: Sidebar background — neutral.100 in light.

border.$description: Right-edge divider — alpha.black.8 in light.

$description.$value: Horizontal top bar spanning the main content area.

bg.$description: Page header background — matches sidebar canvas in light.

border.$description: Bottom divider — alpha.black.8 in light.

$description.$value: Environment switcher widget at the top of the sidebar.

default.$description: Resting background — transparent over sidebar canvas.

hover.$description: Hover fill — alpha.black.6 in light.

open.$description: Background when dropdown is open — neutral.0 in light. Matches dropdown.bg.solid so the trigger reads as one continuous surface with the menu panel (env list / user menu).

border.$description: Bottom separator below the env switcher row — alpha.black.8 in light.

bg.$description: Square logo/avatar container background — neutral.100 in light.

border.$description: Logo container border — alpha.black.8 in light.

label.$description: Static ‘Environment’ label above the name — neutral.500 in light.

default.$description: Environment name text at rest — neutral.900 in light.

hover.$description: Environment name on hover — neutral.950 in light.

chevron.$description: Up/down chevron icon — neutral.500 in light.

$description.$value: Usage/quota stats section pinned above the user row.

bg.$description: Transparent — inherits sidebar panel background.

border.$description: Top separator — alpha.black.10 in light.

default.description: Usage value at rest — neutral.500 in light.

strong.description: Current count when usage is normal.

warning.description: Current count when approaching limit (>80%) — warning.600 in light.

danger.description: Current count at or over limit — danger.600 in light.

$description.$value: Data table tokens. Tables sit on surface.page (neutral.25 in light). Row hover composes two layers: (1) a half-transparent white fill (alpha.white.50) for a 'soft lift' over surface.page, and (2) a 2px left-border accent in interactive.selectedFill (info.700), mirroring the selected-nav stripe pattern. Selected row uses state.selected (neutral.100) — same neutral grey used by item.bg.selected, menu selection, and nav selection. Dark mode keeps state.hover (alpha.white.8) for the bg and the same accent token; alpha values land brighter in dark, so the soft-lift and the stripe both read correctly.
Row-level tokens — background fills for default/hover/selected states and the inter-row separator.

default.$description: Default row — transparent. Inherits surface.page (neutral.25 in light).

hover.$description: Hovered row — half-transparent white (#FFFFFF80) composes onto surface.page (neutral.25) for a soft lift. Pair with table.row.accent.hover on the left edge.

selected.$description: Selected row — neutral.100 in light. Same fill as item.bg.selected, menu.item.bg.selected, and command list selection — keeps the 'selected' language consistent across every list-like surface.

selectedHover.$description: Selected + hover stays stable at neutral.100. Depth is already established; further fill change creates flicker.

$description.$value: Left-edge accent stripe — applied as a 2px left border on the row. Mirrors the selected-nav stripe pattern (interactive.selectedFill). Today only the hover slot is defined; a selected slot can be added later if selection should also get the stripe.

hover.$description: Hover stripe — info.700 in light. 2px left border applied on row hover, alongside table.row.bg.hover.

border.$description: Row separator — alpha.black.6 in light. Applied as a bottom border on each row.

$description.$value: Tab navigation component tokens. Supports Default (pill), Line (underline), and Vertical orientations. Mirror of Dark.json — same token names, different primitive references.

background.$description: Container trough behind pill tabs. neutral.100 in light — active pills sit above this as white.

backgroundLine.$description: Line variant has no container fill — indicator does the work.

border.$description: Optional outer border on the tab list container. alpha.black.8 in light.

default.$description: Inactive tab — no fill, sits inside the container trough.

hover.$description: Hovered inactive tab. alpha.black.6 in light.

active.$description: Selected tab pill fill. neutral.0 (white) in light — clearly raised above panelInset (100) container.

disabled.$description: Disabled tab has no fill — communicated via text.disabled only.

default.$description: Inactive tab label. neutral.600 — 5.9:1 on panelInset (100). ✓ AA.

hover.$description: Hovered tab label. neutral.800 — steps up from secondary on interaction.

active.$description: Selected tab label. neutral.800 — 15.3:1 on panel (white). ✓ AAA.

disabled.$description: Disabled tab label. neutral.400 — WCAG exempts disabled; legible at 2.8:1.

active.$description: Subtle border on active pill tab — helps it read as a card on the light container. alpha.black.8 in light.

color.$description: Line variant active underline. neutral.800 in light — matches active tab text.

colorActive.$description: Aliased for Token Studio component binding clarity.

$description.$value: Pagination component tokens — horizontal page navigation. Four item types (Previous, Next, Link, Ellipsis) share one set of state tokens. Active state = currently selected page (Link items only — Previous/Next never enter active). Mirrors the tabs pattern: lifted panel + border on the active item, transparent + step-up text on inactive items. Mirror of Dark.json.

default.$description: Inactive item — transparent. Sits directly on surface.page (neutral.25).

hover.$description: Hovered item pill. alpha.black.6 in light.

active.$description: Selected page — neutral.0 (white) in light. Lifted from surface.page by fill + border. Same pattern as tabs.item.background.active.

activeHover.$description: Hover on the selected page — alpha.black.6 layered over the panel fill.

disabled.$description: Disabled Previous/Next — no fill. Communicated via text.disabled + icon.disabled.

default.$description: Default item label (Previous, Next, Link). neutral.600 in light — 7.41:1 on surface.page. ✓ AAA.

hover.$description: Hovered/focused label — steps up from secondary. neutral.800 in light.

active.$description: Selected page label. neutral.800 in light — 18.1:1 on panel (white). ✓ AAA.

disabled.$description: Disabled label (e.g. Previous on first page). neutral.400 — WCAG-exempt for disabled.

ellipsis.$description: Non-interactive ellipsis (…). Muted — signals it's not a button.

default.$description: Chevron in Previous/Next at rest. icon/secondary — neutral.500 in light.

hover.$description: Chevron on hover/focus — steps up to icon/default. neutral.700 in light.

active.$description: Active chevron. Previous/Next never enter the active page state, but defined for consistency.

disabled.$description: Disabled chevron — neutral.400 in light.

default.$description: No border on inactive items.

active.$description: Subtle border on the selected page — separates the white panel fill from surface.page. alpha.black.8 in light. Same role as tabs.item.border.active.

$description.$value: Standalone Toggle component tokens — a single two-state button that can be on or off. Two variants: Default (ghost, borderless) and Outline (always-visible border). Three sizes: sm, md, lg. States: off, off+hover, on, on+hover, disabled, focus. Use Toggle for single in/out controls (e.g. 'Bold' in a toolbar). Use ToggleGroup for grouped sets. Mirror of Dark.json.
Background fills. Shared across Default and Outline variants — the variants only differ in border treatment.

off.$description: Off/inactive — no fill. Sits on whatever surface it is placed on.

offHover.$description: Hover on off state. alpha.black.6 in light — composes onto any surface.

on.$description: On/active — neutral.100 in light. Creates a clear 'pressed' look against white panels.

onHover.$description: On + hover. alpha.black.10 composited over the on fill — a touch darker.

disabled.$description: Disabled — communicated via dimmed text/icon, not fill.

off.$description: Off state label. neutral.600 — 6.01:1 on panel (white) ✓ AA.

offHover.$description: Hover on off state — steps up from secondary.

on.$description: On state label. neutral.800 — 12.56:1 on panelInset (100) ✓ AAA.

disabled.$description: Disabled label. neutral.400 — WCAG-exempt.

on.$description: On state icon.

$description.$value: Border tokens separated by variant. Default variant is borderless (ghost), Outline variant always has a visible border. Focus ring is shared.

off.$description: Default variant — ghost button, no border.

on.$description: Default variant stays borderless when on — fill carries the state.

off.$description: Outline variant — always visible. alpha.black.8 in light.

on.$description: On state keeps standard border — fill already carries the signal.

disabled.$description: Disabled outline border.

$description.$value: Toggle Group component tokens. A set of two-state buttons that can be toggled on/off, grouped horizontally or vertically. Supports Default (connected, no trough), Fill (container with trough, equal-width items), and With Spacing (detached items, visible gaps). Item selection can be single or multi. Mirror of Dark.json.
Container wrapper around toggle items. Background is only rendered for the Fill variant — Default and With Spacing variants use a transparent container so items read as the UI.

default.$description: Default + With Spacing variants have no container fill — items carry the visual structure.

fill.$description: Fill variant trough. neutral.100 in light — active item (white) sits above this, matching the pill-tabs pattern.

disabled.$description: Whole group disabled fill. neutral.50 in light.

default.$description: Outer border on Default and Fill containers. alpha.black.8 in light — hairline separator.

spacing.$description: With Spacing variant has no outer container border — each item carries its own.

$description.$value: Individual toggle button within the group. Off = inactive, On = selected/pressed.

off.$description: Inactive item — no fill. Fill variant items rest on panelInset trough; Default/Spacing items rest on page bg.

offHover.$description: Hover on inactive item. alpha.black.6 in light — composes onto any surface.

on.$description: Selected (on) item. neutral.0 (white) in light — pops above the panelInset trough in Fill variant, matches the shadcn pattern.

onHover.$description: Selected + hover — keep stable. Depth is already established; further fill change creates flicker.

disabled.$description: Disabled item — communicated via dimmed text/icon, not fill.

off.$description: Inactive item label. neutral.600 — 5.5:1 on panelInset (100) ✓ AA.

offHover.$description: Hovered inactive item label — steps up from secondary.

on.$description: Selected item label. neutral.800 — 15.3:1 on white ✓ AAA.

disabled.$description: Disabled item label. neutral.400 — WCAG-exempt; legible at 2.8:1.

off.$description: Inactive item icon. Matches text.secondary tone.

offHover.$description: Hovered inactive item icon — steps up.

on.$description: Selected item icon — primary icon tone.

disabled.$description: Disabled item icon.

default.$description: Item border for Default (connected) and With Spacing (detached) variants. alpha.black.8 in light.

on.$description: Selected item border — subtle. Same alpha.black.8 — the fill change carries the state, border stays neutral.

fill.$description: Items in Fill variant have no border — container trough + fill separation do the work.

$description.$value: Selection controls: checkbox, radio, switch.

invalid.$description: Unselected+invalid: bg stays transparent; the border carries the invalid signal.

dangerFill.$description: Danger-filled bg for a selected+invalid control (e.g. checked checkbox in error state).

invalid.$description: Invalid state border — danger.500 in light, danger.500 in dark.

dangerHover.$description: Hover border on invalid control — darker danger stop.

invalid.$description: Icon/checkmark color in invalid unselected state.

$description.$value: Checkbox-specific aliases.

dangerFill.$description: Danger fill for checked+invalid checkbox. References control.bg.dangerFill.

invalidSelected.$description: Checkmark on danger fill — stays white (onBrand) for legibility.

$description.$value: Box variant — wraps control + label + description in a bordered card.

invalid.$description: Decorative tint only — not a text contrast pair.

$description.$value: Radio-specific aliases.
Box variant — wraps radio + label + description in a bordered card.
Switch track and thumb aliases.

off.$description: neutral.450 (#8B8C8F) — 3.36:1 vs white page bg ✓, and 3.36:1 vs white thumb ✓. Both pass WCAG 1.4.11 (3:1 UI components). Previous value neutral.50 gave 1.09:1 — thumb was invisible.

dangerOff.$description: Invalid+Off track — same medium gray as off state. Border carries the danger signal, not the fill.

dangerOn.$description: Invalid+On track turns danger instead of info.

invalid.$description: Outline ring applied to entire switch track when invalid.

$description.$value: Box variant — wraps switch + label + description in a bordered card.

on.$description: Box bg when switch is On — subtle info tint matches selected state.

$description.$value: Alert and toast banner component tokens. Aliases into status.* — mode-specific colors resolved via Primitives.

Chart component tokens — used by usage charts, dashboards, and metric panels (e.g. Billing & usage page). Categorical stacked bars use chart.series.1–6 (decorative-only colours from accent.* + info). Status-based charts should reference status.* colours directly. Both modes share the same token names.
Chart container — the bordered card that wraps the title, value, and plot area.

background.$description: Chart panel background — neutral.0 in light.

border.$description: Hairline border around the chart panel — neutral.200 in light.

title.$description: Chart title (e.g. ‘Connections’, ‘Proxy requests’). Pair with fontSize.md + fontWeight.medium at the Figma text-style level.

value.$description: Large numeric total under the title (e.g. ‘453’, ‘5,453,937’). Pair with fontSize.xl + fontWeight.semibold.

$description.$value: Axis labels and lines along the X (categories/days) and Y (values) edges of the plot area.

label.$description: Axis tick labels — small, low-emphasis. neutral.600 in light. Pair with fontSize.2xs.

line.$description: Axis baseline. Transparent by default — gridlines do the work. Switch to {border.muted} if a visible axis line is required.

gridline.$description: Horizontal gridlines behind the bars. alpha.black.6 in light. Render as a 1px hairline (or dashed if preferred).

1.$description: Primary series — Nango cyan (#00B2E3). First/dominant category in any breakdown.

2.$description: Mint (#6ED6AC). Pairs visually with cyan without competing.

3.$description: Yellow (#F7C752). Decorative — not a warning. Use for the third stacked category, never to communicate caution.

6.$description: Soft blue (#90A1F0). Reserve slot — sixth category. Distinct from the cyan in series.1.

$description.$value: Categorical data series — assigned in order: 1 first, 2 second, etc. Six slots cover the breakdown views in Billing & usage. If a chart needs more than six categories, repeat from 1 with a tonal shift or switch to a sequential ramp. Keep the assignment stable per chart so colours don't shift between renders.
Per-bar styling — applied to each segment in a stacked bar.

separator.$description: 1px line between stacked segments. Same colour as panel background — creates a clean visual gap between segments without a hard border colour.

$description.$value: Tooltip shown on bar hover — surfaces exact values and series labels.

background.$description: Tooltip card background — neutral.0 in light.

title.$description: Tooltip header — the date or category label of the hovered bar.

label.$description: Series name in a tooltip row.

value.$description: Numeric value in a tooltip row.

$description.$value: Legend — colour swatch + label per series. Optional in the current usage page (the breakdown dropdown handles series assignment), but defined here so it's available when a multi-chart view needs one.
Empty state — shown inside the chart panel when there's no data for the selected period.

text.$description: Empty-state message colour (e.g. ‘No data for this period’).

background.$description: No background — the empty state inherits the chart panel surface.
